channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: FAO Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Forest and Landscape Restoration Projects in Africa
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), with financial support from the Government of Germany, has launched applications for the Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants (DBG) 2026. This funding opportunity is designed to empower local organizations working to restore degraded landscapes, strengthen climate resilience, improve biodiversity, and create sustainable livelihood opportunities […]

The post FAO Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Forest and Landscape Restoration Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), with financial support from the Government of Germany, has launched applications for the Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants (DBG) 2026. This funding opportunity is designed to empower local organizations working to restore degraded landscapes, strengthen climate resilience, improve biodiversity, and create sustainable livelihood opportunities across selected African countries.

Through this grant scheme, eligible organizations can access funding ranging from USD 5,000 to USD 50,000 to implement practical restoration initiatives that directly benefit communities and ecosystems.

The initiative forms part of the broader AFR100 (African Forest Landscape Restoration Initiative), which seeks to restore millions of hectares of degraded land across Africa while supporting economic development and environmental sustainability.

About the Supporting AFR100 Programme

The Supporting AFR100 Programme is being implemented by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and funded by the Government of Germany.

The programme aims to strengthen locally led forest and landscape restoration efforts in selected African landscapes by supporting community-based organizations and local actors that are actively involved in restoring degraded lands and promoting sustainable natural resource management.

The programme is currently being implemented in:

  • Democratic Republic of the Congo
  • Kenya
  • Madagascar
  • Malawi
  • Togo
  • United Republic of Tanzania

By supporting grassroots restoration efforts, the programme seeks to build stronger ecosystems, increase climate resilience, improve food security, and generate economic opportunities for local communities.

Funding Available

Under the Direct Beneficiary Grants mechanism, eligible organizations can apply for grants ranging from:

  • Minimum Grant: USD 5,000
  • Maximum Grant: USD 50,000

Funding will be provided in local currency equivalent amounts.

The grants are intended to support practical and measurable restoration interventions that generate positive environmental and socioeconomic outcomes.

Objectives of the Direct Beneficiary Grants

The Direct Beneficiary Grants scheme aims to support organizations that are:

  • Restoring degraded lands
  • Enhancing community resilience
  • Improving livelihoods
  • Promoting sustainable land management
  • Strengthening restoration-based value chains
  • Supporting biodiversity conservation
  • Increasing ecosystem productivity
  • Encouraging climate adaptation and mitigation efforts

The programme recognizes that local communities play a central role in restoring landscapes and protecting natural resources. As such, the funding is designed to strengthen local ownership and leadership in restoration initiatives.

Eligible Activities

Organizations may seek funding for a broad range of restoration-related interventions, including but not limited to:

1. Tree Planting Initiatives

Projects that promote afforestation and reforestation activities to restore degraded lands and improve ecosystem health.

2. Agroforestry Projects

Activities integrating trees into agricultural systems to improve productivity, biodiversity, and climate resilience.

3. Soil and Water Conservation

Initiatives focused on:

  • Erosion control
  • Watershed management
  • Water harvesting
  • Sustainable soil management practices

4. Community Nurseries

Establishment and strengthening of nurseries for producing quality seedlings and supporting restoration efforts.

5. Biodiversity Conservation

Projects aimed at:

  • Protecting native species
  • Restoring habitats
  • Conserving ecosystems
  • Enhancing ecological connectivity

6. Restoration-Based Livelihood Activities

Income-generating activities linked to restoration efforts that support local economic development while protecting natural resources.

7. Sustainable Value Chain Development

Projects that strengthen restoration-related value chains and create economic opportunities for local communities.

Why This Grant Matters

Across Africa, millions of hectares of land have been degraded due to unsustainable land use, deforestation, climate change, and biodiversity loss. Restoration efforts are increasingly recognized as a critical strategy for addressing these challenges.

The AFR100 Programme supports locally driven solutions that can:

  • Improve food security
  • Increase climate resilience
  • Restore ecosystem services
  • Create green jobs
  • Support sustainable agriculture
  • Strengthen rural livelihoods
  • Protect biodiversity
  • Enhance water resources
  • Reduce land degradation

By investing directly in local organizations, the programme ensures that restoration solutions are community-led and adapted to local realities.

Who Should Consider Applying?

This opportunity is particularly suitable for:

  • Community-based organizations
  • Environmental NGOs
  • Conservation organizations
  • Agroforestry groups
  • Farmer associations
  • Rural development organizations
  • Natural resource management groups
  • Restoration-focused cooperatives
  • Biodiversity conservation organizations

Organizations with proven experience implementing restoration activities within the designated programme landscapes are especially encouraged to apply.

Application Process

Before beginning the application process, applicants should carefully review the official Applicant Guidelines to ensure compliance with all eligibility requirements.

Organizations should ensure they:

  1. Confirm eligibility.
  2. Review the Applicant Guidelines thoroughly.
  3. Prepare all required documentation.
  4. Develop a clear and realistic project proposal.
  5. Demonstrate how proposed activities contribute to restoration and community resilience.
  6. Submit their application before the deadline.

Key Benefits of the Programme

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Grant funding between USD 5,000 and USD 50,000
  • Support for locally led restoration initiatives
  • Opportunity to strengthen community resilience
  • Capacity to improve restoration-based livelihoods
  • Contribution to national and continental restoration goals
  • Increased environmental and social impact
  • Enhanced ecosystem restoration outcomes
  • Support from a globally recognized United Nations agency

Important Dates

Application Opening Date

22 May 2026

Application Deadline

19 June 2026 at 11:59 PM (GMT)

Applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

Apply Here 

Need More Information?

Organizations seeking clarification regarding the grant opportunity can contact the programme team during the application period through:

Email: afr-100-programme@fao.org

Prospective applicants should consult the official Applicant Guidelines and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements before submitting their proposals.

The Supporting AFR100 Programme represents an important opportunity for local organizations across Africa to access funding that can help restore degraded landscapes, strengthen livelihoods, and contribute to a more resilient and sustainable future.

For more information on this grant opportunity click here

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY, visit here  

The post FAO Supporting AFR100 Programme Direct Beneficiary Grants 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Forest and Landscape Restoration Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) Presidential Fellow 2026–2027: Earn Up to $200,000 While Working Directly with the President of One of the World’s Leading Science Organizations
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) has opened applications for its highly competitive Presidential Fellow Program, an exceptional opportunity designed for outstanding early-career professionals who want to work at the highest levels of leadership, strategy, innovation, and scientific philanthropy. Based at HHMI Headquarters in Chevy Chase, Maryland, USA, the fellowship places a small cohort of […]

The post Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) Presidential Fellow 2026–2027: Earn Up to $200,000 While Working Directly with the President of One of the World’s Leading Science Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) has opened applications for its highly competitive Presidential Fellow Program, an exceptional opportunity designed for outstanding early-career professionals who want to work at the highest levels of leadership, strategy, innovation, and scientific philanthropy.

Based at HHMI Headquarters in Chevy Chase, Maryland, USA, the fellowship places a small cohort of only 2–3 fellows per year directly within the Office of the President. Unlike traditional fellowships that focus primarily on observation or rotations, HHMI Presidential Fellows are entrusted with leading strategic projects, engaging with senior executives, and helping shape the future direction of one of the world’s most influential scientific organizations.

With a salary ranging from $150,000 to $200,000 annually, this fellowship offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious young professionals interested in science, technology, artificial intelligence, policy, philanthropy, education, communications, and organizational leadership.

About Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI)

Howard Hughes Medical Institute is one of the world’s largest private funders of biomedical research.

The organization supports:

  • Scientists at leading universities
  • Research institutes
  • Hospitals
  • Science education initiatives
  • Film and media projects
  • Artificial intelligence-enabled research programs

HHMI is widely recognized for making long-term investments in talented individuals whose work has the potential to transform science and society.

Its headquarters, located in Maryland near Washington, D.C., houses experts from numerous disciplines, including:

  • Biomedical sciences
  • Investments
  • Communications
  • Human resources
  • Technology
  • Digital production
  • Operations
  • Strategic planning

Through these efforts, HHMI contributes to groundbreaking discoveries and educational initiatives that impact people around the world.

What is the HHMI Presidential Fellow Program?

The Presidential Fellows Program is a prestigious leadership fellowship that places selected fellows directly in the Office of the President.

Fellows work under the guidance of:

  • The President of HHMI
  • The Vice President
  • Chief Operating Officer (COO)
  • Senior Advisor to the President

The fellowship is modeled after the Special Assistant to the President role in government, providing participants with high-level exposure, responsibility, and influence.

Rather than observing projects from the sidelines, fellows actively lead strategic initiatives and contribute directly to organizational decision-making.

Why This Fellowship Stands Out

Very few fellowships offer young professionals the opportunity to operate at the intersection of:

  • Science
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Philanthropy
  • Education
  • Media
  • Technology
  • Institutional Strategy

Participants gain direct access to senior leadership while developing skills that are highly transferable across academia, government, nonprofit organizations, consulting firms, and the private sector.

What Presidential Fellows Will Do

Selected fellows will take ownership of important organizational projects and initiatives.

Key responsibilities include:

1. Lead Cross-Functional Strategic Projects

Fellows will manage projects across multiple departments, including:

  • Science programs
  • Artificial intelligence initiatives
  • Finance
  • Human resources
  • Communications
  • Technology
  • Science education
  • Film and media

2. Drive Strategic Decision-Making

Participants will:

  • Analyze complex issues
  • Scope challenges
  • Develop strategic options
  • Coordinate stakeholders
  • Support executive decision-making

Their role is to move initiatives from concept toward implementation.

3. Manage AI-Powered Workflows

A unique feature of this fellowship is its focus on artificial intelligence.

Fellows will:

  • Utilize AI agents
  • Coordinate Large Language Model (LLM) workflows
  • Accelerate institutional projects through emerging technologies

This makes the fellowship particularly attractive to candidates interested in AI and innovation.

4. Handle Sensitive Executive-Level Projects

Fellows will frequently work on confidential matters requiring:

  • Professional judgment
  • Discretion
  • Strategic thinking
  • Executive communication

5. Pursue Self-Directed Projects

One of the most attractive aspects of the program is the opportunity to dedicate:

10% of work time

to approved self-directed projects aligned with the fellow’s interests and professional goals.

Who Should Apply?

HHMI seeks intellectually curious individuals who can quickly learn across disciplines and thrive in fast-paced environments.

The fellowship welcomes both:

  • Outstanding recent graduates
  • Candidates with advanced degrees

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess:

Required

  • Bachelor’s degree

Preferred but Not Required

Advanced degrees such as:

  • MBA
  • MPP
  • PhD
  • JD
  • Equivalent graduate qualifications

Experience Requirements

Applicants should have:

  • 0–5 years of professional experience

Relevant backgrounds may include:

  • Consulting
  • Public policy
  • Research
  • Strategy
  • Law
  • Operations
  • Technology
  • Management
  • Analytics

Skills and Qualities HHMI Is Looking For

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

Strong Analytical Ability

The ability to understand unfamiliar topics quickly and translate information into actionable recommendations.

AI Fluency

Applicants should have experience using:

  • Artificial intelligence tools
  • Large Language Models (LLMs)
  • AI-assisted workflows
  • AI agents

Exceptional Communication Skills

Candidates must be able to:

  • Write clearly
  • Present complex ideas simply
  • Communicate effectively with senior leaders

Adaptability

The role requires comfort with:

  • Ambiguity
  • Rapidly changing priorities
  • Executive-level environments

Intellectual Curiosity

HHMI values candidates who are interested in multiple fields, including:

  • Science
  • Technology
  • Public policy
  • Education
  • Organizational leadership

Fellowship Duration

The fellowship is a:

Fixed-Term Position

2–3 years

This extended duration allows fellows to contribute meaningfully to institutional priorities and gain significant leadership experience.

Location

The fellowship is based at:

HHMI Headquarters

Chevy Chase

The role is primarily on-site.

Salary and Compensation

One of the most attractive aspects of the fellowship is its highly competitive compensation package.

Annual Salary

USD $150,000 – $200,000

Additional benefits include:

  • Health coverage
  • Retirement plans
  • Paid leave
  • Wellness programs
  • Employee recognition initiatives

Benefits of the HHMI Presidential Fellowship

Selected fellows will gain:

Professional Benefits

  • Executive-level exposure
  • Direct mentorship from senior leaders
  • Strategic leadership experience
  • Cross-sector project management experience

Career Benefits

  • Expanded professional network
  • Strong portfolio of high-impact projects
  • Enhanced leadership credentials
  • Increased career opportunities across sectors

Personal Development Benefits

  • Advanced problem-solving skills
  • AI leadership experience
  • Strategic thinking capabilities
  • Organizational governance exposure

Diversity and Inclusion

HHMI is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and welcomes applications from individuals of diverse backgrounds.

The organization values:

  • Diversity of thought
  • Inclusive leadership
  • Equal opportunity
  • Collaborative work environments

HHMI is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their application through the HHMI Careers Portal.

Applications are currently open.

Official Career Portal

HHMI Careers Page

Applicants are encouraged to prepare a strong application highlighting:

  • Leadership experiences
  • Strategic projects
  • AI-related skills
  • Analytical abilities
  • Communication strengths

Apply Here 

Why You Should Consider This Fellowship

The HHMI Presidential Fellows Program is one of the rare opportunities that places early-career professionals directly at the center of leadership within a globally respected scientific institution.

For individuals interested in shaping the future of science, technology, philanthropy, and artificial intelligence while developing world-class leadership skills, this fellowship offers an exceptional platform for growth and impact.

For more information on this fellowship visit here .

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) Presidential Fellow 2026–2027: Earn Up to $200,000 While Working Directly with the President of One of the World’s Leading Science Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Women, Business and the Law (WBL) 2026 Course: Advancing Global Gender Equality Through Legal Reform and Data-Driven Policy
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Despite major global progress in education, employment, and entrepreneurship, a striking reality remains: no country in the world currently provides women and men with fully equal legal rights. This gap in legal equality continues to restrict women’s economic participation, limits entrepreneurship opportunities, and slows down global economic growth. To address this challenge, the Women, Business […]

The post Women, Business and the Law (WBL) 2026 Course: Advancing Global Gender Equality Through Legal Reform and Data-Driven Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Despite major global progress in education, employment, and entrepreneurship, a striking reality remains: no country in the world currently provides women and men with fully equal legal rights.

This gap in legal equality continues to restrict women’s economic participation, limits entrepreneurship opportunities, and slows down global economic growth.

To address this challenge, the Women, Business and the Law (WBL) 2026 course invites policymakers, researchers, and development professionals to explore global data, legal frameworks, and evidence-based policy solutions aimed at closing the gender equality gap.

This online learning program is built around the World Bank’s Women, Business and the Law index, which examines how legal barriers and implementation gaps affect women’s economic opportunities across 190 economies worldwide.

About the Women, Business and the Law (WBL) Index

The WBL index is a globally recognized research framework that measures how laws impact women’s economic inclusion.

It focuses on:

  • Legal rights affecting women’s work and entrepreneurship
  • Barriers in employment, pay, and mobility
  • Laws affecting marriage, parenthood, and pensions
  • Enforcement gaps between law and implementation
  • Cross-country legal comparisons across 190 economies

By analyzing these dimensions, the WBL framework provides policymakers with evidence needed to design effective gender equality reforms.

Course Overview: Women, Business and the Law 2026

The Women, Business and the Law 2026 course is an e-learning program designed to equip participants with the knowledge and tools needed to understand, analyze, and apply gender equality data in policymaking.

Key Course Details

  • Duration: 15 hours total learning time
  • Video content: 2 hours
  • Format: Online e-learning platform
  • Community access: Multiple discussion forums
  • Assessment: Included
  • Certification: Available upon completion
  • Enrollment status: Open
  • Start date for registered users: 23 June 2026

What You Will Learn

The course is designed to build both analytical and practical policy skills. Participants will learn how to:

1. Understand Legal Barriers to Women’s Economic Participation

  • Identify laws that restrict women’s access to economic opportunities
  • Explore key legal constraints across different sectors
  • Analyze gender gaps in legal systems globally

2. Apply the Women, Business and the Law Index

  • Understand the methodology behind the WBL index
  • Learn how data is collected and interpreted
  • Explore the index’s three core pillars

3. Use Data for Policy Development

  • Translate research findings into actionable policy recommendations
  • Support government reforms using data-driven evidence
  • Improve decision-making in ministries and public institutions

4. Analyze Global and Regional Trends

  • Compare legal frameworks across countries
  • Identify regions with strongest and weakest performance
  • Understand reform patterns over time

Course Structure and Modules

The course is structured into five key learning sections:

Module 1: Introduction to the Course

  • Welcome overview
  • Pre-course assessment
  • Introduction to WBL framework
  • Keynote sessions
  • Forum discussions

Module 2: Understanding the WBL Index

  • Global trends from WBL 2026
  • Three pillars of the index
  • Knowledge checks and readings

Module 3: Women’s Rights Across the Life Cycle

  • Legal rights from employment to retirement
  • Deep dives into weak-performing legal areas
  • Reform case studies and podcasts

Module 4: Using WBL Data

  • Exploring WBL databases
  • Economy profiles and reform tracking
  • Policy application and decision-making tools

Module 5: Course Completion

  • Final assessment
  • Certification
  • Feedback and next steps

Who Should Enroll in This Course?

This course is designed for professionals involved in policy, governance, research, and development.

Target Participants Include:

  • Government officials
  • Central bank and financial sector staff
  • Ministry employees (Economy, Finance, Gender, Social Protection, Labor)
  • Policy analysts and advisors
  • Legal professionals
  • Economists and researchers
  • Civil society organizations (CSOs)
  • Academic professionals

Learning Outcomes

By completing this course, participants will be able to:

  • Identify key legal constraints affecting women’s economic participation
  • Use global datasets to inform policy decisions
  • Develop evidence-based gender equality reforms
  • Strengthen cross-country comparative analysis skills
  • Improve policy briefing and advisory capabilities

Course Benefits

Participants will gain:

  • Access to world-class gender equality data
  • Practical policy design skills
  • Internationally recognized learning certification
  • Networking opportunities through discussion forums
  • Exposure to global best practices in legal reform

Why This Course Is Important

Gender inequality in law remains one of the biggest barriers to inclusive economic growth. By equipping professionals with data-driven tools and legal insights, the Women, Business and the Law 2026 course helps strengthen:

  • Economic inclusion
  • Women’s empowerment
  • National policy reform
  • Sustainable development goals (SDGs)

Enrollment Information

  • Enrollment: Open now
  • Start Date: 23 June 2026

Click here for more and to apply

Final Note

This course provides a rare opportunity to engage with global gender equality data and transform it into meaningful policy action. Whether you are a policymaker, researcher, or development practitioner, this program equips you with the tools needed to help close the gender gap in economic opportunity worldwide.

Click here for more global Training Programs

 

The post Women, Business and the Law (WBL) 2026 Course: Advancing Global Gender Equality Through Legal Reform and Data-Driven Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026: Win Global Recognition, Acceleration Support, and Scale Your Ocean Innovation Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Innovators, entrepreneurs, researchers, startups, companies, NGOs, and institutions working on sustainable ocean and water solutions now have an exciting opportunity to showcase their innovations on a global stage. The UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026 is officially accepting applications from organizations and innovators developing transformative solutions that contribute to a more sustainable, resilient, and inclusive […]

The post UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026: Win Global Recognition, Acceleration Support, and Scale Your Ocean Innovation Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Innovators, entrepreneurs, researchers, startups, companies, NGOs, and institutions working on sustainable ocean and water solutions now have an exciting opportunity to showcase their innovations on a global stage.

The UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026 is officially accepting applications from organizations and innovators developing transformative solutions that contribute to a more sustainable, resilient, and inclusive Blue Economy.

Organized by the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) Investment and Technology Promotion Office Italy and the World Trade Centers Association (WTCA), this international competition seeks innovative technologies and scalable business solutions capable of addressing some of the world’s most pressing ocean, coastal, and freshwater challenges.

Selected winners will gain access to a prestigious acceleration program, international visibility, expert mentoring, business matchmaking opportunities, potential pilot projects, and global networking opportunities through UNIDO and WTCA’s extensive international ecosystem.

Understanding the Blue Economy

The Blue Economy represents all economic activities linked to oceans, seas, rivers, lakes, and other water resources while ensuring environmental sustainability and ecosystem protection.

According to the OECD, the Blue Economy generated approximately $1.5 trillion globally in 2010 and is projected to:

  • Double in size by 2030.
  • Reach approximately $5 trillion in gross value added by 2050.
  • Support the livelihoods of more than 3 billion people worldwide.

Beyond its economic significance, the Blue Economy plays a critical role in:

  • Climate regulation.
  • Biodiversity conservation.
  • Food security.
  • Coastal protection.
  • Sustainable development.
  • Renewable energy generation.

As global demand for sustainable development increases, innovative solutions are becoming essential to balance economic growth with environmental protection.

About the UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026

The Global Call aims to identify, support, and accelerate innovative solutions that contribute to sustainable Blue Economy development.

The initiative specifically seeks:

  • Ready-to-deploy technologies.
  • Scalable innovations.
  • Sustainable business models.
  • High-impact solutions.
  • Inclusive development initiatives.
  • Climate-resilient technologies.

Particular emphasis will be placed on innovations that benefit:

  • Developing countries.
  • Economies in transition.
  • Small Island Developing States (SIDS).
  • Coastal communities.
  • Vulnerable populations dependent on marine and freshwater resources.

Six Strategic Categories

Applicants may select up to two categories when submitting their proposal.

1. Blue Renewable Energy

Solutions that promote sustainable energy generation from marine and aquatic resources, including:

  • Offshore wind energy.
  • Wave energy technologies.
  • Tidal power systems.
  • Ocean thermal energy solutions.
  • Hybrid renewable energy systems.

2. Sustainable Blue Food

Innovations that support responsible food production from aquatic environments, including:

  • Sustainable aquaculture.
  • Fisheries innovation.
  • Seafood traceability technologies.
  • Alternative aquatic food systems.
  • Smart fishery management solutions.

3. Green Maritime Transport and Smart Ports

Solutions that reduce environmental impacts across maritime logistics and shipping sectors, including:

  • Green shipping technologies.
  • Smart port systems.
  • Maritime digitalization.
  • Emission reduction solutions.
  • Sustainable logistics innovations.

4. Coastal and Marine Tourism & Recreation

Solutions that promote sustainable tourism while protecting marine ecosystems and maximizing economic benefits for local communities.

Potential focus areas include:

  • Eco-tourism technologies.
  • Sustainable destination management.
  • Coastal conservation initiatives.
  • Community-based tourism models.
  • Digital tools for responsible tourism.

5. Sea, Lakes and River Health & Restoration

Innovations designed to restore and protect aquatic ecosystems, including:

  • Water restoration technologies.
  • Pollution monitoring systems.
  • Plastic waste reduction solutions.
  • Ecosystem rehabilitation initiatives.
  • Biodiversity protection technologies.

6. Blue Tech & Biotechnology

Advanced technologies and scientific innovations supporting sustainable aquatic development, including:

  • Marine biotechnology.
  • Ocean data analytics.
  • Artificial intelligence applications.
  • Bio-based solutions.
  • Marine resource optimization technologies.

Why Participate?

The UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026 offers significantly more than recognition.

Winning projects will receive access to a comprehensive acceleration ecosystem designed to help innovations scale internationally.

Benefits for Winners

Each category winner will enter a dedicated acceleration program under the UNIDO-WTCA umbrella and receive:

UNIDO Support

  • Technical cooperation expertise.
  • Promotion in partner countries.
  • Potential pilot and implementation opportunities.
  • Access to development cooperation networks.

WTCA Support

  • Global business development platforms.
  • International market access opportunities.
  • Client and investor matchmaking.
  • Business acceleration support.
  • Worldwide visibility through WTCA’s network.

Strategic Growth Support

  • High-level mentoring.
  • Expert guidance.
  • Scaling support.
  • International networking opportunities.
  • One year of post-award support.

Investment and Capital-Raising Opportunities

In addition to category winners, up to ten shortlisted applicants in each category will receive support through:

  • Virtual demo days.
  • In-person investor showcases.
  • Investment portfolio publications.
  • Exposure to development finance institutions.
  • Venture capital networks.
  • Impact investors.
  • Commercial banks.
  • Philanthropic organizations.

Travel Support

Winning teams attending the Award Ceremony in Rome may receive reimbursement of up to USD $3,000 for eligible travel and accommodation expenses associated with participation in the event.

Who Can Apply?

The Global Call welcomes applications from:

  • Startups.
  • MSMEs.
  • Large companies.
  • Universities.
  • Research institutions.
  • NGOs.
  • Public-private partnerships.
  • Foundations.
  • Innovation initiatives.
  • Technology developers.

Applicants may submit:

  • Early-stage innovations.
  • Pilot projects.
  • Scaling solutions.
  • Technologies undergoing testing.
  • Proven commercial innovations.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  1. Applicants must be at least 18 years old.
  2. Applications must be submitted in English.
  3. Applicants may select up to two categories.
  4. Projects must demonstrate a Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of 4 or higher.
  5. Applications must be complete and aligned with the objectives of the competition.
  6. Solutions must demonstrate feasibility and credibility.
  7. Entries must be original and owned by the applicant.
  8. No modifications can be made after submission.

Evaluation Criteria

Applications will be assessed by a Scientific Committee and an International Jury based on the following criteria:

Evaluation Area Weight
Innovative Content 25%
Team Competitiveness 20%
Scalability Potential 15%
Technology Readiness Level 10%
Environmental, Social & Economic Impact 10%
Expected Employment Generation 10%
Financial Sustainability 10%

Timeline

Application Opening

20 April 2026

Application Deadline

19 June 2026

Evaluation and Selection Period

June – July 2026

Award Ceremony and Showcase

1 October 2026

The award ceremony will take place in Rome, Italy during the Euro Mediterranean Water Forum.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Why This Opportunity Matters

As oceans, rivers, lakes, and coastal ecosystems face increasing environmental pressures, innovative solutions are essential for building a sustainable future.

The UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026 represents a unique opportunity for innovators worldwide to showcase their technologies, gain international recognition, connect with investors, and accelerate solutions that can improve livelihoods while protecting critical aquatic ecosystems.

For startups, researchers, NGOs, and enterprises working to transform the Blue Economy, this competition offers a powerful platform to scale impact globally.

Application Deadline: 19 June 2026

Click here for more global Competition opportunities

 

The post UNIDO-WTCA Blue Economy Global Call 2026: Win Global Recognition, Acceleration Support, and Scale Your Ocean Innovation Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mixed Methods Researcher at SaverLife: Join a Mission-Driven Team Transforming Financial Futures Across America
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Are you passionate about uncovering meaningful insights through research and transforming data into stories that inspire action? SaverLife is currently seeking a talented Mixed Methods Researcher to join its growing team. This exciting opportunity is ideal for professionals who thrive at the intersection of science, storytelling, analytics, and human-centered impact. As a mission-driven nonprofit organization, […]

The post Mixed Methods Researcher at SaverLife: Join a Mission-Driven Team Transforming Financial Futures Across America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about uncovering meaningful insights through research and transforming data into stories that inspire action? SaverLife is currently seeking a talented Mixed Methods Researcher to join its growing team. This exciting opportunity is ideal for professionals who thrive at the intersection of science, storytelling, analytics, and human-centered impact.

As a mission-driven nonprofit organization, SaverLife is dedicated to helping individuals and families save more money, reduce financial stress, and build stronger financial futures. The organization is now looking for a researcher who can lead projects from inception to completion, turning complex research questions into actionable insights that support positive change.

If you enjoy designing studies, analyzing data, uncovering trends, and communicating findings in compelling ways, this role offers an opportunity to make a meaningful impact while working with a purpose-driven organization.

About SaverLife

SaverLife is a nonprofit organization focused on empowering individuals to achieve greater financial stability and resilience. Through research, innovation, and member-focused programs, the organization helps people develop stronger savings habits and build brighter futures.

The SaverLife team is united by a common mission: helping members save more, worry less, and improve their financial well-being. The organization values curiosity, ambition, agility, and a people-first mindset in everything it does.

By joining SaverLife, employees become part of a collaborative community that is committed to creating measurable social impact.

Mixed Methods Researcher Position Overview

The Mixed Methods Researcher role is designed for professionals who can seamlessly blend qualitative and quantitative research approaches. SaverLife describes the ideal candidate as someone who is equally a scientist and a storyteller.

This position involves managing the entire research lifecycle, from identifying research questions to presenting findings and recommendations to stakeholders.

Researchers in this role will be expected to:

  • Design rigorous and evidence-based research studies.
  • Collect and analyze both qualitative and quantitative data.
  • Work with complex and sometimes messy datasets.
  • Extract meaningful insights from research findings.
  • Translate data into compelling narratives.
  • Present findings to stakeholders in a clear and actionable manner.
  • Manage multiple research projects from start to finish.
  • Adapt quickly as organizational priorities evolve.
  • Ensure accuracy, quality, and attention to detail throughout the research process.

What Makes This Opportunity Unique?

Unlike many research positions that focus solely on one stage of the research process, this role offers ownership of the entire project lifecycle.

Successful candidates will have the opportunity to:

1. Lead Research from Start to Finish

Researchers will be responsible for:

  • Defining research objectives.
  • Designing methodologies.
  • Conducting data collection.
  • Performing analysis.
  • Creating reports.
  • Delivering presentations and recommendations.

2. Influence Decision-Making

The insights generated through research will directly contribute to organizational strategies and programs that support financial well-being for SaverLife members.

3. Combine Science and Storytelling

The role is ideal for professionals who enjoy both technical research work and communicating findings in ways that inspire action.

4. Work in a Mission-Driven Environment

Employees contribute to a meaningful cause that helps people improve their financial security and quality of life.

The Ideal Candidate

SaverLife is searching for individuals who:

  • Love asking questions and exploring complex problems.
  • Enjoy both qualitative and quantitative research methods.
  • Are comfortable working with ambiguity.
  • Can manage competing priorities effectively.
  • Value precision and attention to detail.
  • Communicate findings clearly to diverse audiences.
  • Thrive in collaborative environments.
  • Have a strong passion for social impact and evidence-based decision-making.

Candidates who enjoy uncovering patterns in data and translating them into practical recommendations will find this position especially rewarding.

Benefits of Working at SaverLife

SaverLife believes that a rewarding career should support both professional development and personal well-being.

As a remote-first organization, SaverLife provides a comprehensive benefits package that supports employees in multiple aspects of their lives.

Health and Wellness Benefits

Employees receive:

  • Full medical coverage.
  • Full dental coverage.
  • Full vision coverage.
  • 50% family coverage support.

Work-Life Balance Benefits

SaverLife offers:

  • Flexible work schedules.
  • Alternating Flex Fridays off.
  • Generous vacation leave.
  • Generous sick leave.
  • 11 paid holidays annually.

Professional Development Opportunities

Employees receive:

  • $1,000 annual professional development stipend.
  • Opportunities to enhance research and professional skills.
  • Support for continuous learning and career growth.

Financial Benefits

SaverLife provides:

  • Competitive compensation.
  • 401(k) matching up to 5% annually.

Remote Work Support

To help employees build productive home workspaces, SaverLife offers:

  • $500 home office setup stipend.

SaverLife’s Core Values

The organization’s culture is built around four key values:

People First

Prioritizing the needs and experiences of both members and team members.

Agile By Design

Remaining adaptable and responsive to changing needs and opportunities.

Curious

Encouraging continuous learning, exploration, and innovation.

Ambitious

Pursuing meaningful impact through bold goals and measurable outcomes.

These values guide decision-making, collaboration, and organizational growth.

Why Consider This Role?

The Mixed Methods Researcher position presents a rare opportunity to:

  • Conduct meaningful research that influences real-world outcomes.
  • Work with a mission-driven nonprofit organization.
  • Enjoy the flexibility of a remote-first work environment.
  • Receive strong benefits and professional development support.
  • Lead impactful projects from concept to completion.
  • Collaborate with a team committed to improving financial well-being nationwide.

For professionals who are passionate about research, storytelling, and social impact, this position offers a unique platform to make a difference while advancing their careers.

Application Information

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications as soon as possible.

Click here for more and to apply

Candidates should review the complete job description and application requirements before submitting their applications.

Click here for more global job opportunities.

 

The post Mixed Methods Researcher at SaverLife: Join a Mission-Driven Team Transforming Financial Futures Across America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SHE-CREEDS Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded MSc and PhD Opportunities for African Students in Climate-Responsive Agriculture, Energy-Efficient Technologies, and Digital Innovation
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
African students seeking world-class postgraduate education, international academic exposure, and the opportunity to contribute to sustainable development now have an exceptional opportunity through the SHE-CREEDS Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scholarships 2026. Co-funded by the European Union under the Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scheme (NDICI-2024-MOBAF), the SHE-CREEDS project is offering fully supported Master’s and PhD scholarship opportunities for […]

The post SHE-CREEDS Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded MSc and PhD Opportunities for African Students in Climate-Responsive Agriculture, Energy-Efficient Technologies, and Digital Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
African students seeking world-class postgraduate education, international academic exposure, and the opportunity to contribute to sustainable development now have an exceptional opportunity through the SHE-CREEDS Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scholarships 2026.

Co-funded by the European Union under the Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scheme (NDICI-2024-MOBAF), the SHE-CREEDS project is offering fully supported Master’s and PhD scholarship opportunities for qualified students from Sub-Saharan Africa. The initiative is designed to strengthen higher education systems, promote academic mobility, foster regional integration, and equip future leaders with the knowledge and skills needed to address climate change, food security, renewable energy challenges, and digital transformation across Africa.

The scholarship programme is coordinated by the Nelson Mandela African Institution of Science and Technology (NM-AIST) in Tanzania and implemented through a consortium of leading universities across Eastern, Central, Southern, and Western Africa, with technical support from Ghent University in Belgium and collaboration from the Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) in Ghana.

About the SHE-CREEDS Project

SHE-CREEDS stands for:

Strengthening Higher Education for Climate-Responsive Agriculture, Energy-Efficient Technologies, and Digital Innovation in Sub-Saharan Africa.

The project is a four-year initiative running from 2025 to 2028 under the Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scheme managed by the European Education and Culture Executive Agency (EACEA) on behalf of the European Union.

The programme seeks to strengthen the capacity of African higher education institutions to respond effectively to climate change and sustainable development challenges through postgraduate education, research, innovation, and cross-border academic mobility.

The project focuses on:

  • Mainstreaming climate change into postgraduate education.
  • Improving the quality of training in Climate-Responsive Agriculture.
  • Advancing Energy-Efficient Technologies.
  • Accelerating digitalisation in higher education.
  • Promoting regional integration among African universities.
  • Strengthening food security and sustainable agriculture.
  • Supporting renewable energy development.
  • Enhancing graduate employability and green skills.
  • Building a resilient and environmentally sustainable Africa.

Through academic mobility and knowledge exchange, SHE-CREEDS aims to create a new generation of African researchers, innovators, and professionals capable of driving the continent’s green transition.

Why the SHE-CREEDS Scholarship Matters

Africa faces growing challenges related to climate change, environmental sustainability, food security, energy access, and technological advancement. Addressing these challenges requires highly trained professionals, researchers, and innovators equipped with interdisciplinary knowledge and practical skills.

The SHE-CREEDS programme responds directly to these needs by investing in advanced postgraduate education and supporting mobility between African institutions. Scholars gain access to diverse academic environments, international networks, advanced research facilities, and opportunities to collaborate across borders.

The programme aligns with the European Union’s Global Gateway priorities and supports long-term capacity building across African higher education institutions.

Scholarship Opportunities Available

PhD Scholarships

The programme offers six PhD scholarship opportunities.

Available PhD Programmes

  1. NM-AIST, Tanzania
    • PhD in Sustainable Agriculture
    • Focus: Agro-Ecological Farming Systems and Sustainable Land Management
    • Credit-Seeking Mobility (6 Months)
  2. Jimma University, Ethiopia
    • PhD in Natural Resources Management
    • Specialisation: Climate Change Adaptation and Mitigation
    • Focus: Smart Irrigation, Water-Energy-Food Nexus, and Precision Agriculture Technologies
    • Degree-Seeking
  3. Université Officielle de Bukavu (UOB), Democratic Republic of Congo
    • PhD in Environmental Science – Natural Resource Management
    • Focus:
      • Agro-Industrial Waste Valorisation
      • Biodiversity-Positive Farming
      • Sustainable Mining for Land Rehabilitation in the Albertine Rift
    • Degree-Seeking
  4. Université Félix Houphouët-Boigny (UFHB), Côte d’Ivoire
    • PhD in Climate Change and Biodiversity
    • Focus: Climate-Smart Cocoa Landscapes
    • Degree-Seeking
  5. NM-AIST, Tanzania
    • PhD in Hydrology and Water Resources Engineering
    • Focus:
      • Smart Irrigation
      • Water-Energy-Food Nexus
      • Precision Agriculture Technologies
    • Credit-Seeking Mobility (6 Months)
  6. NM-AIST, Tanzania
    • PhD in Sustainable Energy Science and Engineering
    • Focus:
      • Renewable Energy Systems
      • Green Manufacturing
      • Solar-Driven Technologies
    • Degree-Seeking
  7. University of Zambia (UNZA), Zambia
    • PhD in Integrated Water Resources Management
    • Focus:
      • Energy Efficiency in Transport
      • Water Infrastructure
      • Urban Systems
      • Energy-Efficient Water Systems
    • Degree-Seeking

MSc Scholarships

The programme offers Master’s scholarship opportunities in multiple specialised fields.

Available MSc Programmes

  1. NM-AIST, Tanzania
    • MSc in Sustainable Agriculture
    • Focus: Agro-Ecological Farming Systems and Sustainable Land Management
  2. Jimma University, Ethiopia
    • MSc in Agronomy
    • Focus:
      • Agrifood Value Chain Development
      • Rural SME Empowerment
  3. Université Officielle de Bukavu (DRC)
    • MSc in Natural Resource Management
    • Focus:
      • Agro-Industrial Waste Valorisation
      • Biodiversity-Positive Farming
      • Sustainable Mining for Land Rehabilitation
  4. Université Félix Houphouët-Boigny, Côte d’Ivoire
    • MSc in Climate Change, Biodiversity and Sustainable Agriculture
    • Focus:
      • Agro-Ecological Farming Systems
      • Sustainable Land Management
  5. University of Zambia
    • Master of Science in Geology
    • Focus:
      • Mineral Systems
      • Hydrogeology
      • Geological Hazard Assessment
  6. NM-AIST, Tanzania
    • MSc in Sustainable Energy Science and Engineering
    • Focus:
      • Renewable Energy Systems
      • Green Manufacturing
      • Solar-Driven Technologies

Scholarship Benefits

Successful candidates will receive comprehensive support throughout their studies.

Financial Benefits

MSc Scholars

  • Monthly stipend of up to EUR 890.
  • Funding duration of up to 24 months for degree-seeking programmes.

PhD Scholars

  • Monthly stipend of up to EUR 1,230.
  • Funding duration of up to 36 months for degree-seeking programmes.
  • Funding duration of up to 6 months for credit-seeking mobility.

Additional Benefits

  • International academic mobility opportunities.
  • Access to leading African universities.
  • Research collaboration opportunities.
  • Exposure to multicultural academic environments.
  • Professional networking opportunities.
  • Enhanced employability and leadership development.
  • Participation in research addressing real African development challenges.
  • Opportunities to contribute to climate resilience, renewable energy development, and digital innovation.

Partner Universities and Institutions

The SHE-CREEDS consortium consists of distinguished institutions across Africa and Europe.

Consortium Members

  • Nelson Mandela African Institution of Science and Technology (NM-AIST), Tanzania – Lead Coordinator.
  • Jimma University (JU), Ethiopia.
  • Université Officielle de Bukavu (UOB), Democratic Republic of Congo.
  • Université Félix Houphouët-Boigny (UFHB), Côte d’Ivoire.
  • University of Zambia (UNZA), Zambia.
  • Ghent University (UGent), Belgium – EU Technical Partner.
  • Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), Ghana – Associated Partner.

Cross-Language Mobility Advantage

The programme encourages linguistic diversity and regional integration.

Applicants from Anglophone countries applying to Francophone institutions (UOB or UFHB) receive a cross-language selection advantage. Similarly, applicants from Francophone countries applying to Anglophone institutions may benefit from this initiative.

Language support services will be available upon arrival at host institutions.

Important Mobility Requirement

Applicants cannot study in their country of nationality or residence.

The scholarship is specifically designed to promote cross-border academic mobility. Therefore:

  • The host institution must be located in a different African country from the applicant’s nationality.
  • The host institution must also be located outside the applicant’s country of residence.
  • Applications proposing in-country mobility will be automatically disqualified.

Gender Inclusion and Support

SHE-CREEDS strongly promotes gender equality.

The project aims to achieve at least 50% female scholarship recipients and offers:

  • A 5-point bonus during selection.
  • Dedicated mentorship programmes.
  • Female peer-support networks.
  • Family visit allowances for degree-seeking PhD scholars.
  • Flexible arrangements for nursing mothers.

Priority Groups

Priority consideration is given to:

  • Applicants from disadvantaged socio-economic backgrounds.
  • Applicants from conflict-affected and fragile regions.
  • Underrepresented groups in STEM fields.
  • Cross-language applicants.
  • Persons with disabilities requiring accessible support.

Application Process

Applicants should follow these steps:

Step 1

Review available MSc and PhD programmes and identify suitable opportunities.

Step 2

Prepare all required supporting documents in PDF format.

Step 3

Complete the official online application form.

Step 4

Submit the application before the deadline.

Step 5

Participate in a virtual interview if shortlisted.

Step 6

Await final selection results, expected by July 2026.

Required Application Documents

All Applicants

  • Completed online application.
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  • Motivation Letter.
  • Valid Passport.
  • National Identity Card.
  • Academic Certificates.
  • Academic Transcripts.
  • Language Proficiency Evidence (where applicable).
  • Signed Declaration confirming no concurrent EU scholarship and no previous MOBAF scholarship.

Additional Documents for MSc Applicants

  • Two Academic or Professional Reference Letters.
  • Statement of Purpose.

Additional Documents for PhD Applicants

  • Research Concept Note (1,000–1,500 words).
  • Two Academic or Professional Reference Letters.
  • Research Publications, Reports, or Conference Papers (if available).

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

28 June 2026 at 23:59 GMT

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

How to Apply

To learn more and apply: Read the official call for application

The SHE-CREEDS scholarship programme represents one of Africa’s most significant postgraduate mobility initiatives, offering students the opportunity to study across borders, engage in transformative research, and become leaders in climate-responsive agriculture, energy-efficient technologies, and digital innovation. Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare their applications early and submit before the official deadline.

For more information, visit the official website
Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post SHE-CREEDS Intra-Africa Academic Mobility Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded MSc and PhD Opportunities for African Students in Climate-Responsive Agriculture, Energy-Efficient Technologies, and Digital Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic 2026 for Student Journalists in Ghana and The Gambia: Fully-Focused Training Opportunity for Emerging Media Leaders
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic, an exciting capacity-building opportunity designed for aspiring student journalists in Ghana and The Gambia. Organized through the Next Gen (Campus Reporter) Project, this initiative seeks to equip young journalists, storytellers, communicators, and campus media practitioners […]

The post Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic 2026 for Student Journalists in Ghana and The Gambia: Fully-Focused Training Opportunity for Emerging Media Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic, an exciting capacity-building opportunity designed for aspiring student journalists in Ghana and The Gambia.

Organized through the Next Gen (Campus Reporter) Project, this initiative seeks to equip young journalists, storytellers, communicators, and campus media practitioners with the practical skills, ethical foundations, and digital tools needed to excel in today’s rapidly evolving media landscape.

The programme aims to nurture a new generation of media professionals capable of producing impactful journalism that promotes accountability, inclusion, democratic values, transparency, and social development across their campuses and communities.

The application deadline is Monday, 8 June 2026.

About the Next Gen (Campus Reporter) Project

The Next Gen Project, also known as the Campus Reporter Project, is a learning and mentorship platform developed by the Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development.

The initiative focuses on empowering young media practitioners across tertiary institutions in West Africa by providing training, mentorship, and exposure to modern journalism practices.

The programme recognizes the growing importance of:

  • Ethical journalism
  • Digital storytelling
  • Data-driven reporting
  • Fact-checking
  • Investigative journalism
  • Multimedia communication
  • Information integrity

By strengthening these competencies, the project helps young journalists become more effective contributors to democratic governance and community development.

Why the Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic Matters

The media landscape is changing rapidly due to technological advancements, social media expansion, misinformation challenges, and the growing demand for credible information.

Student journalists often serve as important voices within their institutions and local communities. However, many lack access to specialized training and mentorship opportunities.

The Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic addresses this gap by providing participants with practical skills that will help them:

  • Produce accurate and ethical journalism
  • Combat misinformation and disinformation
  • Amplify underrepresented voices
  • Use digital tools effectively
  • Develop investigative reporting skills
  • Create impactful multimedia content
  • Promote accountability and transparency

Training Focus Areas

Selected participants will undergo intensive practical training covering several critical journalism competencies.

1. Journalism Ethics and Responsible Reporting

Participants will learn the principles that guide professional journalism, including:

  • Accuracy
  • Fairness
  • Objectivity
  • Accountability
  • Public interest reporting

The training will emphasize responsible reporting practices that strengthen public trust in journalism.

2. Gender-Sensitive Reporting and Inclusive Storytelling

The programme will help participants understand how to report stories in ways that:

  • Promote inclusion
  • Reduce harmful stereotypes
  • Reflect diverse perspectives
  • Elevate marginalized voices

Participants will learn techniques for producing more equitable and representative journalism.

3. Data Journalism and Story Interpretation

Data is becoming increasingly important in modern journalism.

Participants will learn how to:

  • Analyze data
  • Interpret statistics
  • Identify trends
  • Use data to support stories
  • Present complex information clearly

These skills can help journalists create evidence-based reporting that informs audiences effectively.

4. Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Techniques

OSINT has become a vital tool for journalists worldwide.

Participants will be introduced to methods for:

  • Gathering publicly available information
  • Verifying online content
  • Conducting digital investigations
  • Monitoring developments across multiple platforms

These skills are increasingly valuable for investigative and digital reporting.

5. Fact-Checking and Verification

In an era of misinformation and disinformation, verification skills are essential.

The programme will train participants to:

  • Verify information sources
  • Identify misleading content
  • Conduct fact-checking investigations
  • Strengthen information integrity

These competencies help ensure accuracy and credibility in journalism.

6. Optimal Use of Social Media and Search Engines for Story Amplification

Participants will learn strategies for:

  • Growing audience engagement
  • Increasing story visibility
  • Using search engines effectively
  • Leveraging social media platforms professionally
  • Expanding the reach of impactful journalism

This training helps journalists maximize the impact of their reporting.

7. Digital Storytelling and Multimedia Reporting

Modern audiences consume content across multiple formats.

Participants will gain practical knowledge in:

  • Multimedia journalism
  • Visual storytelling
  • Digital content creation
  • Interactive reporting
  • Audience-centered communication

These skills can help student journalists adapt to contemporary media environments.

8. Reporting on Governance, Human Rights, and Social Issues

The clinic will also focus on reporting topics that shape communities and societies, including:

  • Governance
  • Human rights
  • Social justice
  • Civic participation
  • Community development

Participants will learn approaches for covering sensitive issues responsibly and effectively.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated using several key considerations.

Demonstrated Interest in Journalism

Applicants should show genuine enthusiasm for journalism, storytelling, media, and communication.

Quality of Responses

The application form includes written questions that allow applicants to demonstrate:

  • Critical thinking
  • Communication skills
  • Understanding of journalism principles

Strong, thoughtful responses will strengthen an application.

Previous Media Experience

Applicants with experience in:

  • Campus media
  • Student newspapers
  • Blogging
  • Storytelling
  • Podcasting
  • Content creation

may have an advantage, although experience is not necessarily mandatory.

Commitment to Ethical and Inclusive Journalism

The programme prioritizes applicants who demonstrate a strong commitment to:

  • Ethical reporting
  • Diversity and inclusion
  • Responsible journalism
  • Community impact

Potential for Community Impact

Applicants should show how they intend to apply the knowledge gained to benefit:

  • Their campuses
  • Local communities
  • Student audiences
  • Broader society

Gender Balance

The programme also seeks balanced representation and encourages applications from diverse groups.

Who Can Apply?

This opportunity is specifically designed for students interested in journalism and media practice.

Applicants must:

  • Be students currently enrolled in a tertiary institution
  • Have an interest in journalism, media, storytelling, or communication
  • Be based in Ghana or The Gambia
  • Be willing to fully participate in the programme

Application Requirements

Applicants will be required to provide information including:

Personal Information

  • Full name
  • Email address
  • Phone number
  • Gender
  • Nationality
  • State or region

Academic Information

  • Name of institution
  • Course of study
  • Level or year of study

Student Identification

Applicants must upload:

  • School ID card
  • Or another valid form of student identification

Journalism Experience

Applicants will be asked about:

  • Campus press membership
  • Published content
  • Journalism training attended
  • Storytelling experience

Written Responses

Applicants must answer questions such as:

  • Why they want to participate
  • How ethical and inclusive journalism can improve society

These responses play an important role in the selection process.

Benefits of Participating

Selected participants will gain:

Practical Journalism Training

Hands-on learning from experienced media professionals.

Digital Journalism Skills

Exposure to modern reporting tools and techniques.

Networking Opportunities

Connections with fellow student journalists and media practitioners.

Enhanced Employability

Valuable skills that strengthen future careers in:

  • Journalism
  • Communications
  • Media
  • Public relations
  • Digital storytelling
  • Development communication

Mentorship Opportunities

Access to guidance and support from journalism professionals.

Application Deadline

Monday, 8 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline.

Apply Here

Interested applicants can explore the Next Gen/Campus Reporter Project here

Discover more training opportunities on OFY here 

 

The post Next Gen Campus Journalism Clinic 2026 for Student Journalists in Ghana and The Gambia: Fully-Focused Training Opportunity for Emerging Media Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Applications Open for the 2026 Global Survivor Council in Manila, Philippines: Join Global Advocacy Efforts to End Violence Against Children
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Brave Movement has officially opened applications for the 2026 Global Survivor Council (GSC) ahead of the highly anticipated 2nd Global Ministerial Conference on Ending Violence Against Children, which will take place in Manila. This important international opportunity seeks individuals from survivor-led networks and communities worldwide who are passionate about ending violence against children and ensuring […]

The post Applications Open for the 2026 Global Survivor Council in Manila, Philippines: Join Global Advocacy Efforts to End Violence Against Children appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Brave Movement has officially opened applications for the 2026 Global Survivor Council (GSC) ahead of the highly anticipated 2nd Global Ministerial Conference on Ending Violence Against Children, which will take place in Manila.

This important international opportunity seeks individuals from survivor-led networks and communities worldwide who are passionate about ending violence against children and ensuring survivor voices are represented in global policy discussions and advocacy initiatives.

The Global Survivor Council will play a central role in shaping the design and delivery of the conference while strengthening survivor-led advocacy and international collaboration around child protection and violence prevention.

Applications close on 10 June 2026.

About Brave Movement

Brave Movement is a global survivor-led advocacy movement dedicated to ending childhood sexual violence and protecting children from abuse, exploitation, and violence.

The movement works with survivors, advocates, policymakers, organizations, and communities around the world to:

  • Raise awareness about violence against children
  • Amplify survivor voices
  • Advocate for prevention and protection policies
  • Promote survivor-centered solutions
  • Strengthen global accountability and action

Brave Movement has become an important international platform for survivor leadership and advocacy in the movement to end childhood violence.

About the Global Survivor Council (GSC)

The Global Survivor Council (GSC) is a global body bringing together individuals from survivor-led networks and organizations worldwide who can contribute lived experience, advocacy expertise, and insights on different forms of violence against children.

The Council is being coordinated by Brave Movement as part of preparations for the:

2nd Global Ministerial Conference on Ending Violence Against Children

The Council will help ensure that survivor perspectives remain central to global conversations, policies, and conference activities.

Purpose of the Global Survivor Council

The Global Survivor Council aims to:

  • Ensure survivor voices shape global advocacy discussions
  • Contribute to conference planning and implementation
  • Promote survivor-centered approaches
  • Strengthen global collaboration among survivor-led movements
  • Advance action to end violence against children worldwide

The initiative recognizes that survivors play a critical role in shaping effective prevention, response, and protection strategies.

Conference Location

The 2nd Global Ministerial Conference on Ending Violence Against Children will take place in:

Manila

The conference will bring together:

  • Governments
  • International organizations
  • Civil society groups
  • Survivor advocates
  • Researchers
  • Youth leaders
  • Child protection experts

The goal is to strengthen international cooperation and action to prevent violence against children globally.

Duration of the Global Survivor Council

The Global Survivor Council will be active from:

June 2026 – November 2026

Selected participants will engage in advocacy, consultations, planning discussions, and conference-related activities throughout this period.

What the Global Survivor Council Will Do

Council members will contribute to several important activities, including:

1. Survivor-Centered Advocacy

Members will help ensure survivor experiences and perspectives are reflected in conference discussions and outcomes.

2. Global Consultation and Collaboration

Participants will collaborate with survivor-led networks and international stakeholders from different regions of the world.

3. Conference Design and Delivery

Council members may contribute ideas, feedback, and guidance related to:

  • Conference themes
  • Programming
  • Sessions and discussions
  • Advocacy priorities
  • Survivor participation strategies

4. Strengthening Global Action

The Council will help shape international conversations on:

  • Child protection
  • Violence prevention
  • Survivor support
  • Policy reform
  • Advocacy initiatives

Benefits of Participating

Although membership on the Global Survivor Council is voluntary, selected members will receive important support and opportunities.

Key Benefits Include:

1. Global Advocacy Experience

Participants will contribute to a major international conference focused on ending violence against children.

2. International Networking Opportunities

Members will connect with:

  • Survivor advocates
  • International organizations
  • Policymakers
  • Child protection experts
  • Civil society leaders
  • Youth advocates

3. Stipend Support

Council members will receive:

A stipend acknowledging their participation

This support recognizes the time and contributions of selected members.

4. Travel Support

For participants invited to attend the conference in Manila:

  • Travel expenses will be covered
  • Per diem support will be provided

This helps ensure broader participation and accessibility for global members.

Who Should Apply?

The opportunity is open to individuals connected to survivor-led networks and advocacy communities worldwide.

Applicants should ideally:

  • Have lived experience relevant to violence against children advocacy
  • Be engaged in survivor-led initiatives or networks
  • Be passionate about child protection and violence prevention
  • Be interested in global advocacy and policy discussions
  • Be committed to survivor-centered approaches

The Council seeks diverse global representation and perspectives.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Violence against children remains one of the world’s most urgent human rights and public health challenges.

The Global Survivor Council represents an important step toward:

  • Centering survivor leadership
  • Strengthening accountability
  • Promoting prevention
  • Building inclusive policy solutions
  • Supporting global advocacy movements

By involving survivors directly in international dialogue and conference planning, the initiative aims to ensure more effective, informed, and compassionate responses to violence against children.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 10 June 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply before the official closing date.

How to Apply

Interested individuals can learn more and apply through the official application link shared by Brave Movement:

Apply Here 

Applicants can also learn more about the organization through here 

Discover more about this fellowship opportunity on here

The post Applications Open for the 2026 Global Survivor Council in Manila, Philippines: Join Global Advocacy Efforts to End Violence Against Children appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FCDO Senior Research Fellowships 2027: Fully Funded Two-Year Fellowship Opportunities Across 11 Global Research Areas
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO), through the Global Research and Technology Development (GRTD) Research Commissioning Centre (RCC), has officially opened applications for eleven prestigious Senior Research Fellowships for 2027. These fellowships are designed to connect leading academic researchers with UK government policymaking and international development initiatives. The programme is co-led by the International […]

The post FCDO Senior Research Fellowships 2027: Fully Funded Two-Year Fellowship Opportunities Across 11 Global Research Areas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO), through the Global Research and Technology Development (GRTD) Research Commissioning Centre (RCC), has officially opened applications for eleven prestigious Senior Research Fellowships for 2027. These fellowships are designed to connect leading academic researchers with UK government policymaking and international development initiatives.

The programme is co-led by the International Initiative for Impact Evaluation (3ie) and the University of Birmingham and offers experienced researchers the opportunity to contribute directly to high-impact global research and foreign policy priorities.

Applications are now open, and interested candidates must submit their applications before 30 August 2026 at 5 PM GMT.

About the FCDO Senior Research Fellowships

The FCDO Senior Research Fellowships are aimed at senior academics who want to apply their expertise to real-world international challenges while collaborating closely with policymakers and government teams.

The fellowships are part of the Global Research and Technology Development (GRTD) initiative, which supports evidence-based policymaking and strengthens the impact of research in development and foreign policy.

Successful fellows will work with the FCDO on strategic research themes that influence global policy discussions, international development programmes, geopolitical strategies, technological advancement, and resilience planning.

The placements will be hosted at the FCDO offices in London or East Kilbride.

Fellowship Thematic Areas

Applicants can apply under one of the following eleven thematic areas:

  1. Gender and Equity
  2. Conflict
  3. AI for Development
  4. Food and Agriculture
  5. Economic Development
  6. Systemic Resilience of Combined Human, Social, Economic, Political and Ecological Systems
  7. Hybrid and Transnational Threats
  8. Polar Regions
  9. Critical Minerals
  10. Technology, Geopolitics and Information Manipulation
  11. Global Health

These themes reflect major global priorities shaping international development, diplomacy, technology, sustainability, and security.

Fellowship Duration and Commitment

The fellowship placement will run for:

  • Two years
  • 0.4 Full-Time Equivalent (FTE)

This structure allows fellows to remain employed by their home academic institution while contributing substantially to FCDO-led research and policy initiatives.

Fellowship Location

Selected fellows will be hosted by the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) at:

  • London, United Kingdom
  • East Kilbride, United Kingdom

Placement locations are subject to successful security clearance approval.

Key Dates

Important dates for applicants include:

  • Call for Applications Opened: 20 May 2026
  • Application Deadline: 30 August 2026 at 5 PM GMT
  • Interviews: Early October 2026
  • Fellowship Start Date: 1 April 2027

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early application submission is strongly recommended.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the FCDO Senior Research Fellowships, applicants must meet the following criteria:

Academic Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be at least at Associate Professor level or equivalent seniority
  • Be employed by a UK academic institution
  • Remain employees of their sending institution throughout the fellowship period

Security Clearance Requirements

Applicants must be eligible to obtain UK security clearance.

Depending on the fellowship role, applicants may require either:

Developed Vetting (DV)

Applicants must have physically lived in the UK for at least 5 of the last 10 years.

Security Clearance (SC)

Applicants must have physically lived in the UK for at least 2 of the last 5 years.

The programme emphasizes that physical residency in the UK is mandatory. Maintaining a UK bank account or postal address alone does not satisfy residency requirements.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The FCDO Senior Research Fellowship programme offers a rare opportunity for experienced academics to bridge the gap between research and policymaking.

Benefits of the fellowship include:

  • Direct engagement with UK foreign policy and development initiatives
  • Opportunity to shape international policy discussions
  • Collaboration with government teams and policymakers
  • Contribution to high-impact interdisciplinary research
  • Access to strategic global development networks
  • Enhanced professional visibility and influence
  • Opportunity to work on globally significant challenges

This fellowship is especially valuable for researchers interested in policy impact, international development, geopolitical strategy, technology governance, and global systems resilience.

About the GRTD Research Commissioning Centre (RCC)

The Research Commissioning Centre (RCC) was established to streamline and improve the commissioning and management of impactful research for the FCDO.

The RCC helps reduce administrative barriers in research funding and delivery while ensuring high-quality, policy-relevant research outcomes.

The centre is jointly led by:

  • 3ie (International Initiative for Impact Evaluation)
  • University of Birmingham

Together, these institutions support evidence-informed policymaking and development impact worldwide.

Required Policies and Compliance

Shortlisted and selected fellows will be required to comply with several FCDO and 3ie policies, including:

  • FCDO Senior Research Fellowship Agreement Template
  • FCDO Programme Operating Framework (PrOF)
  • Conflict of Interest and Publication Guidance
  • 3ie Safeguarding Policy
  • 3ie Fraud and Anti-Corruption Policy
  • 3ie Transparent, Reproducible, and Ethical Evidence (TREE) Policy

Applicants are strongly encouraged to review these policies carefully before submitting their applications.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Review the detailed job description for their selected thematic area
